fields.definition_en: To leave something somewhere; to put money into a bank account.
fields.example_en: I need to deposit some money in my savings account.
fields.example_vi: Tôi cần gửi một số tiền vào tài khoản tiết kiệm của mình.
fields.mnemonic: De- (down) + posit (place). To put money down in the bank.

fields.definition_en: To take something, especially something necessary or pleasant, away from someone.
fields.example_en: He claimed that he had been deprived of his freedom.
fields.example_vi: Anh ta tuyên bố rằng anh ta đã bị tước đoạt tự do.
fields.mnemonic: De- (away) + prive (private/own). To take away what someone owns.

fields.definition_en: A person who is given the power to do something instead of another person, or the person whose rank is immediately below that of the leader of an organization.
fields.example_en: I'm acting as deputy while the manager is away.
fields.example_vi: Tôi đang làm phó quản lý trong khi quản lý đi vắng.
fields.mnemonic: A deputy is deputized to do the duties of the boss.

fields.definition_en: To go or come down.
fields.example_en: The path descends steeply into the valley.
fields.example_vi: Con đường đi xuống dốc đứng vào thung lũng.
fields.mnemonic: De- (down) + scend (climb). To climb down.

fields.definition_en: The state or fact of being related to a particular person or group of people who lived in the past; or a movement down.
fields.example_en: He claims to be of royal descent.
fields.example_vi: Anh ta tự nhận mình có nguồn gốc hoàng gia.
fields.mnemonic: Noun form of descend. Also refers to your ancestors (coming down the family tree).

fields.definition_en: To choose someone officially to do a particular job.
fields.example_en: She has been designated to organize the meeting.
fields.example_vi: Cô ấy đã được chỉ định để tổ chức cuộc họp.
fields.mnemonic: De-SIGN-ate. To give someone a sign or name for a job.

fields.definition_en: Worth having and wanted by most people.
fields.example_en: It's highly desirable that you attend the meeting.
fields.example_vi: Rất mong muốn bạn tham dự cuộc họp.
fields.mnemonic: Able to be desired.

fields.meaning_vi: máy tính để bàn, màn hình chính
fields.definition_en: A view on a computer screen that contains icons representing files, programs, and other features of the computer; or a computer that fits on a desk.
fields.example_en: I saved the file to my desktop.
fields.example_vi: Tôi đã lưu tệp vào màn hình chính của mình.
fields.mnemonic: A computer that sits on top of a desk.

fields.definition_en: Causing, or able to cause, damage.
fields.example_en: The storm was highly destructive.
fields.example_vi: Cơn bão có tính hủy diệt cao.
fields.mnemonic: Adjective form of destroy.

fields.definition_en: To force someone officially to stay in a place.
fields.example_en: A suspect has been detained by the police for questioning.
fields.example_vi: Một nghi phạm đã bị cảnh sát giam giữ để thẩm vấn.
fields.mnemonic: De- (down) + tain (hold). To hold someone down/back.

fields.definition_en: The fact of noticing or discovering something.
fields.example_en: Early detection of the disease is crucial for successful treatment.
fields.example_vi: Phát hiện sớm căn bệnh là rất quan trọng để điều trị thành công.
fields.mnemonic: Noun form of detect. What a detective does.

fields.meaning_vi: sự giam giữ, phạt cấm túc
fields.definition_en: The act of officially detaining someone; or a form of punishment in which children are made to stay at school for a short time after classes have ended.
fields.example_en: The suspect is currently in police detention.
fields.example_vi: Nghi phạm hiện đang bị cảnh sát giam giữ.
fields.mnemonic: Noun form of detain.

fields.meaning_vi: làm xấu đi, tồi tệ hơn
fields.definition_en: To become worse.
fields.example_en: Her health began to deteriorate rapidly.
fields.example_vi: Sức khỏe của cô ấy bắt đầu tồi tệ đi nhanh chóng.
fields.mnemonic: To tear (ter) something down makes its condition deteriorate.

fields.definition_en: To destroy a place or thing completely or cause great damage.
fields.example_en: The town was devastated by a hurricane in 1928.
fields.example_vi: Thị trấn đã bị tàn phá bởi một cơn bão vào năm 1928.
fields.mnemonic: To make a VAST area dead is to DE-VAST-ATE it.

fields.definition_en: An evil being, often represented in human form but with a tail and horns.
fields.example_en: He sold his soul to the devil.
fields.example_vi: Hắn ta đã bán linh hồn mình cho ác quỷ.
fields.mnemonic: Devil = D (do) + evil.

fields.definition_en: To invent a plan, system, object, etc., usually using your intelligence or imagination.
fields.example_en: He devised a new method for testing the software.
fields.example_vi: Anh ấy đã nghĩ ra một phương pháp mới để kiểm tra phần mềm.
fields.mnemonic: To make a DEVICE, you must first DEVISE a plan.

fields.definition_en: To recognize and name the exact character of a disease or a problem, by examining it.
fields.example_en: She was diagnosed with diabetes.
fields.example_vi: Cô ấy được chẩn đoán mắc bệnh tiểu đường.
fields.mnemonic: Dia- (through) + gnosis (knowledge). To know the illness through examining.

fields.definition_en: A judgment about what a particular illness or problem is, made after examining it.
fields.example_en: The doctor made a quick diagnosis of the infection.
fields.example_vi: Bác sĩ đã đưa ra một chẩn đoán nhanh chóng về tình trạng nhiễm trùng.
fields.mnemonic: Noun form of diagnose.

fields.meaning_vi: tuyên bố, ra lệnh, đọc chính tả
fields.definition_en: To give orders, or tell someone exactly what they must do, with total authority.
fields.example_en: The terms of the treaty were dictated by the winning side.
fields.example_vi: Các điều khoản của hiệp ước được đưa ra bởi phe chiến thắng.
fields.mnemonic: Dictators dictate.

fields.definition_en: A leader who has complete power in a country and has not been elected by the people.
fields.example_en: The country was ruled by a military dictator for decades.
fields.example_vi: Đất nước đã bị cai trị bởi một nhà độc tài quân sự trong nhiều thập kỷ.
fields.mnemonic: A person who dictates to everyone.

fields.definition_en: To show or find the difference between things that are compared.
fields.example_en: We need to differentiate between fact and opinion.
fields.example_vi: Chúng ta cần phân biệt giữa thực tế và ý kiến.
fields.mnemonic: To make or see the DIFFERENT-iation.

fields.definition_en: The importance and value that a person has, that makes other people respect them or makes them respect themselves.
fields.example_en: He maintained his dignity despite the criticism.
fields.example_vi: Anh ấy vẫn giữ được phẩm giá của mình bất chấp những lời chỉ trích.
fields.mnemonic: A person with dignity digs deeply into themselves to act right.

fields.definition_en: A situation in which a difficult choice has to be made between two different things you could do.
fields.example_en: She faced a moral dilemma over whether to tell the truth.
fields.example_vi: Cô ấy phải đối mặt với tình thế tiến thoái lưỡng nan về đạo đức về việc có nên nói ra sự thật hay không.
fields.mnemonic: Di (two) + lemma (premise). You have two choices, and both are bad.

fields.definition_en: A measurement of something in a particular direction; or a part or feature or way of considering something.
fields.example_en: Please specify the dimensions of the room.
fields.example_vi: Vui lòng ghi rõ kích thước của căn phòng.
fields.mnemonic: 3D movies are in 3 Dimensions.

fields.definition_en: To reduce or be reduced in size or importance.
fields.example_en: I don't want to diminish her achievements, but she did have a lot of help.
fields.example_vi: Tôi không muốn làm giảm bớt thành tựu của cô ấy, nhưng cô ấy thực sự đã có rất nhiều sự giúp đỡ.
fields.mnemonic: Di-MINI-sh -> making something mini (smaller).

fields.definition_en: To put something into a liquid for a short time; or to go down to a lower level.
fields.example_en: House prices dipped slightly in the summer.
fields.example_vi: Giá nhà đã giảm nhẹ vào mùa hè.
fields.mnemonic: Dip your chip in the dip.

fields.definition_en: An official whose job is to represent one country in another, and who usually works in an embassy.
fields.example_en: The diplomat negotiated a peace treaty between the two nations.
fields.example_vi: Nhà ngoại giao đã đàm phán một hiệp ước hòa bình giữa hai quốc gia.
fields.mnemonic: A diplomat uses diplomacy to get things done.

fields.definition_en: Involving diplomats or the management of the relationships between countries; or acting in a way that does not cause offense.
fields.example_en: We need a diplomatic solution to this crisis.
fields.example_vi: Chúng ta cần một giải pháp ngoại giao cho cuộc khủng hoảng này.
fields.mnemonic: Adjective form of diplomat.

fields.definition_en: A book that gives a list of names, addresses, or other facts; or a place in a computer system where files are kept.
fields.example_en: You can find his number in the telephone directory.
fields.example_vi: Bạn có thể tìm thấy số của anh ấy trong danh bạ điện thoại.
fields.mnemonic: A directory DIRECTS you to the right person or file.

fields.definition_en: Extremely bad or unsuccessful.
fields.example_en: The consequences of the mistake were disastrous.
fields.example_vi: Hậu quả của sai lầm là một thảm họa.
fields.mnemonic: Adjective form of disaster.

fields.definition_en: To throw something away or get rid of it because you no longer want or need it.
fields.example_en: Discard any old batteries carefully.
fields.example_vi: Hãy vứt bỏ bất kỳ loại pin cũ nào một cách cẩn thận.
fields.mnemonic: Dis (away) + card. Throw away a playing card you don't need.

fields.meaning_vi: xuất viện, thải ra, giải ngũ
fields.definition_en: To allow someone officially to leave somewhere, especially a hospital or a law court; or to send out a substance, especially waste liquid or gas.
fields.example_en: Patients were discharged from the hospital because the beds were needed.
fields.example_vi: Các bệnh nhân đã được xuất viện vì cần giường bệnh.
fields.mnemonic: Dis (opposite) + charge (load). To unload or let go.

fields.definition_en: To make something known publicly, or to show something that was hidden.
fields.example_en: The company has disclosed profits of over £200 million.
fields.example_vi: Công ty đã tiết lộ mức lợi nhuận hơn 200 triệu bảng Anh.
fields.mnemonic: Dis (opposite of) + close. To open up or reveal what was closed.

fields.definition_en: The act of making something known or the fact that is made known.
fields.example_en: Any public disclosure of this information would be very damaging.
fields.example_vi: Bất kỳ sự tiết lộ công khai nào về thông tin này sẽ rất có hại.
fields.mnemonic: Noun form of disclose.

fields.definition_en: Communication in speech or writing.
fields.example_en: The book explores political discourse in the media.
fields.example_vi: Cuốn sách khám phá các diễn ngôn chính trị trên các phương tiện truyền thông.
fields.mnemonic: Discourse is how we DISCUSS things of COURSE.

fields.meaning_vi: sự thận trọng, quyền quyết định
fields.definition_en: The ability to behave without causing embarrassment or attracting too much attention; or the right to choose what should be done in a particular situation.
fields.example_en: Viewer discretion is advised due to violent content.
fields.example_vi: Khán giả nên cân nhắc khi xem do có nội dung bạo lực.
fields.mnemonic: Discrete (kín đáo) -> Discretion (sự thận trọng/kín đáo).

fields.definition_en: Treating a person or particular group of people differently, especially in a worse way from the way in which you treat other people.
fields.example_en: The law prohibits discrimination on the basis of race or gender.
fields.example_vi: Luật pháp nghiêm cấm sự phân biệt đối xử dựa trên chủng tộc hoặc giới tính.
fields.mnemonic: Noun form of discriminate.

fields.meaning_vi: sự sa thải, sự bác bỏ
fields.definition_en: The situation in which an employer officially makes someone leave their job; or a decision that someone or something is not important.
fields.example_en: Unfair dismissal can lead to legal action.
fields.example_vi: Sa thải không công bằng có thể dẫn đến hành động pháp lý.
fields.mnemonic: Noun form of dismiss.

fields.definition_en: To force something or someone out of its usual or original position.
fields.example_en: The building of a new dam will displace thousands of people.
fields.example_vi: Việc xây dựng một con đập mới sẽ khiến hàng nghìn người phải dời chỗ ở.
fields.mnemonic: Dis (away) + place -> To take away someone's place.

fields.meaning_vi: sự vứt bỏ, quyền tùy ý sử dụng
fields.definition_en: The act of getting rid of something, especially by throwing it away.
fields.example_en: The safe disposal of nuclear waste is a major problem.
fields.example_vi: Việc vứt bỏ an toàn rác thải hạt nhân là một vấn đề lớn.
fields.mnemonic: Noun form of dispose.

fields.definition_en: To get rid of something, especially by throwing it away.
fields.example_en: How did they dispose of the body?
fields.example_vi: Họ đã xử lý cái xác như thế nào?
fields.mnemonic: Dis-pose. Put it away from its pose (position).

fields.definition_en: An argument or disagreement, especially an official one between, for example, workers and employers or two countries with a common border.
fields.example_en: They have been unable to settle the dispute over working conditions.
fields.example_vi: Họ đã không thể giải quyết cuộc tranh chấp về điều kiện làm việc.
fields.mnemonic: Dis (apart) + pute (think). Thinking differently leads to a dispute.

fields.definition_en: To prevent something, especially a system, process, or event, from continuing as usual or as expected.
fields.example_en: Heavy snow disrupted travel into the city this morning.
fields.example_vi: Tuyết rơi dày đã làm gián đoạn việc đi lại vào thành phố sáng nay.
fields.mnemonic: Dis (apart) + rupt (break). To break apart an activity.

fields.definition_en: The action of preventing something, especially a system, process, or event, from continuing as usual or as expected.
fields.example_en: The strike caused severe disruption to flights.
fields.example_vi: Cuộc đình công đã gây ra sự gián đoạn nghiêm trọng đối với các chuyến bay.
fields.mnemonic: Noun form of disrupt.

fields.definition_en: To be absorbed by a liquid, especially when mixed; or to end an official organization or a legal arrangement.
fields.example_en: Dissolve two spoons of powder in warm water.
fields.example_vi: Hòa tan hai thìa bột trong nước ấm.
fields.mnemonic: Dis-SOLVE. The solid solves into the liquid.

fields.meaning_vi: sự khác biệt, sự xuất sắc
fields.definition_en: A difference between two similar things; or the quality of being excellent.
fields.example_en: There's a clear distinction between the dialects spoken in the two regions.
fields.example_vi: Có một sự khác biệt rõ ràng giữa các phương ngữ được nói ở hai khu vực.
fields.mnemonic: Distinct (khác biệt) -> Distinction (sự khác biệt, nổi bật).

fields.definition_en: Something that is distinctive is easy to recognize because it is different from other things.
fields.example_en: She's got a very distinctive voice.
fields.example_vi: Cô ấy có một giọng nói rất đặc biệt.
fields.mnemonic: Distinctive things STAND OUT from the rest.

fields.definition_en: To change the shape of something so that it looks strange or unnatural; or to change something so that it is false or wrong.
fields.example_en: The newspaper was accused of distorting the truth.
fields.example_vi: Tờ báo bị cáo buộc đã xuyên tạc sự thật.
fields.mnemonic: Dis (away) + tort (twist) -> To twist out of shape.

fields.meaning_vi: sự đau buồn, tình trạng khẩn cấp
fields.definition_en: A feeling of extreme worry, sadness, or pain; or a situation in which you are suffering or are in great danger and therefore in urgent need of help.
fields.example_en: The ship sent out a distress signal.
fields.example_vi: Con tàu đã phát ra một tín hiệu cấp cứu.
fields.mnemonic: Dis-STRESS. Extreme stress.

fields.meaning_vi: gây lo âu, đáng lo ngại
fields.definition_en: Making you feel worried or upset.
fields.example_en: The article described the disturbing trend of cyberbullying.
fields.example_vi: Bài báo mô tả xu hướng đáng lo ngại của bắt nạt trên mạng.
fields.mnemonic: Adjective form of disturb. Something that disturbs you.
